text:
There was once a tiny village named Whimsy Hollow. You could hear fairy laughter from every hill. In the center stood an ancient oak. Near its base lived old Oakley.

Every spring Oakley would sing. His song would tell stories. One bird would talk about adventure. Another about love. A clever squirrel collected tales. Oakley knew all their names.

One day, Oakley sang about a mouse who dreamed of flying. The owl didn't believe. The next morning, there was just an ear.

Oakley smiled. So the mouse learned crafts. He made a paper owl. They fell beloved.

On Halloween, the villagers wore the owl. They flew!

Years passed, and Oakley had tales to gallop centuries. Now Oakley just listened. In his heart, he flanted wings.

That was the story they made.<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
